assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration that lacks granular maintenance exclusion types
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'No channel' is legacy and provides detailed comparison table showing missing granular maintenance exclusion types like 'no minor or node upgrades' and 'no minor upgrades'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that 'No channel' does not support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion scope — only the 30-day 'no upgrades' type, AND per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is also limited
assertions.reason: Comparison table clearly shows 'No channel' only supports 30-day 'no upgrades' type and lacks 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion, and states per-nodepool exclusions are limited to 'no upgrades' 30 days

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Comparison table shows Extended support (24 months) as available for release channels but not for No channel, and text mentions 'up to 24 months with Extended support vs. 30-day max on No channel'

assertions.expectation: Notes that rollout sequencing (an advanced feature) is not available on 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Comparison table shows rollout sequencing as available for release channels but not for No channel, and mentions 'Progressive rollout capability — rollout sequencing across your 8-cluster fleet (advanced feature)' as a benefit after migration

assertions.expectation: Recommends Regular or Stable channel as the closest migration target (legacy behavior matches Stable for minors, Regular for patches)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Regular channel as closest match to 'No channel' behavior but does not explain that legacy behavior matches Stable for minors and Regular for patches - it only states Regular channel matches the same minor upgrade pace

assertions.expectation: Mentions Extended channel as a migration path for customers who want maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response mentions Extended channel as an option 'if you want maximum EoS flexibility (delays enforcement until end of extended support)' in the channel selection section

assertions.expectation: Warns about exclusion translation: when moving from 'No channel' to release channels, some exclusions don't translate 1:1 and may be ignored — only 'no_upgrades' type translates between the two. Recommends adding a temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion during the transition
assertions.reason: Response includes explicit step to 'Add a temporary no upgrades exclusion to prevent immediate auto-upgrades' before migration and later removes it, demonstrating awareness of exclusion translation issues

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides the specific gcloud command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel regular'
Response does not reference or mention the specific cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/release-channels URL
